title: Query loses “merge sorted streams” optimization when querying through a VIEW over Distributed table
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Describe the unexpected behaviour
When running an ORDER BY … DESC LIMIT query directly on a Distributed table, ClickHouse uses the efficient plan with
Sorting (Merge sorted streams … for ORDER BY).
However, running the logically equivalent query through a VIEW defined as a simple projection over the same Distributed table (e.g. CREATE VIEW v AS SELECT … FROM distributed_table) produces a different plan:
•	extra VIEW-expansion steps (Convert VIEW subquery result…, Materialize constants…)
•	loss of sorted-stream property
•	fallback to Sorting (Sorting for ORDER BY) above the read
This results in a significantly slower query, despite identical SQL semantics and data sources.
### Which ClickHouse versions are affected?
Latest
### How to reproduce
```
DROP DATABASE IF EXISTS repro ON CLUSTER '{cluster}';
CREATE DATABASE repro ON CLUSTER '{cluster}';
-- Local replicated table
CREATE TABLE repro.events_shard ON CLUSTER '{cluster}'
(
org_id String,
event_time DateTime,
id UInt64,
msg String
)
ENGINE = ReplicatedMergeTree
PARTITION BY toYYYYMMDD(event_time)
ORDER BY (org_id, event_time, id);
-- Distributed table
CREATE TABLE repro.events ON CLUSTER '{cluster}' AS repro.events_shard
ENGINE = Distributed('{cluster}', repro, events_shard, cityHash64(org_id));
-- View that is "just select from the distributed table"
CREATE VIEW repro.events_view ON CLUSTER '{cluster}' AS
SELECT org_id, event_time, id, msg
FROM repro.events;
-- Data
INSERT INTO repro.events
SELECT
toString(number % 100) AS org_id,
now() - (number % 200000) AS event_time,
number AS id,
concat('m', toString(number)) AS msg
FROM numbers(2000000);
-- A) Direct distributed query
EXPLAIN
SELECT id, event_time, msg
FROM repro.events
WHERE event_time >= now() - INTERVAL 2 HOUR AND event_time <= now()
ORDER BY event_time DESC
LIMIT 200;
-- B) Same query through the view
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_db.sample_table (id INTEGER, name VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP, score FLOAT);
CREATE VIEW test_db.sample_view AS SELECT id, name, created_at FROM test_db.sample_table WHERE score > 50.0;
INSERT INTO test_db.sample_table SELECT number, CONCAT('Name', CAST(number AS VARCHAR)), NOW(), number % 100 FROM (SELECT ROW_NUMBER() OVER () AS number FROM rdf::get_data(100));
key_bug_features: Querying through a VIEW loses merge sorted streams optimization
